title: Orange You Glad It's Vitamin C Day?
story: Every April 4, people celebrate National Vitamin C Day by loading up on citrus fruits and healthy snacks! Families squeeze fresh orange juice, munch on strawberries, and share colorful fruit salads. Schools often serve extra oranges at lunch, while health enthusiasts post pictures of their vitamin-packed smoothies online. It's a delicious way to boost your immune system!
activity_question: What do families commonly do to celebrate National Vitamin C Day?
activity_choices: Squeeze fresh orange juice and eat fruit salads
Plant orange trees in their backyard
Wear orange clothing all day long
activity_answer: Squeeze fresh orange juice and eat fruit salads
bonus_fact: One orange contains more than 100% of your daily vitamin C needs!

title: Fancy French Food Gets Its Day
story: Chefs everywhere roll up their sleeves on April 4 for National Cordon Bleu Day! This special day celebrates the famous dish where chicken gets stuffed with ham and cheese, then breaded and fried until golden. Restaurants serve their best versions while home cooks try making this tricky French recipe themselves.
activity_question: What gets stuffed inside the chicken in cordon bleu?
bonus_fact: "Cordon bleu" means "blue ribbon" in French, like a cooking prize!

title: National Walk Around Things Day Gets Silly
story: Every April 4, people celebrate National Walk Around Things Day by doing exactly what it sounds like - walking around stuff instead of through it! Instead of cutting through the park, you stroll around its edges. Rather than walking through doorways normally, you might take the long way around furniture or trees. Some families make it a game, timing how long it takes to walk around their house, car, or even the grocery store. Social media fills with videos of people taking the scenic route around everything from puddles to playground equipment. It's a wonderfully weird way to slow down and notice your surroundings while getting extra steps in your day.
activity_question: What do people do differently on National Walk Around Things Day?
activity_choices: Walk around objects instead of through or past them
Walk only in straight lines all day
activity_answer: Walk around objects instead of through or past them
follow_up_question: What's the biggest thing in your neighborhood you could walk around?
